text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Yuen, Margaret
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ATTENDING ONCOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED]
SERVICE: Surgical Pathology -- Anatomic Pathology
SPECIMEN TYPE: CT-guided core needle biopsy
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [REDACTED]
DATE RECEIVED: [REDACTED]
DATE REPORTED: [REDACTED]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY:
Patient is a 63-year-old East Asian female, former smoker (12 pack-year history, quit 18 years prior), presenting with a 3-month history of progressive cough, two episodes of hemoptysis, and unintentional weight loss. CT chest demonstrated a right upper lobe mass measuring 5.8 x 4.2 cm with associated ipsilateral hilar, mediastinal, and right supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. PET-CT confirms hypermetabolic right upper lobe mass (SUVmax 14.3) with N3 nodal involvement. No evidence of distant metastatic disease on staging workup. Brain MRI negative for intracranial metastases. Procedure performed by interventional radiology under CT guidance. Clinical indication: tissue diagnosis of right upper lobe mass for definitive histologic characterization and molecular profiling.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
A: Right upper lobe mass -- CT-guided core needle biopsy (3 cores in formalin)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and "right upper lobe mass, core biopsy." Three tan-white, firm cylindrical tissue cores measuring 0.9 cm, 1.1 cm, and 0.8 cm in length, each approximately 1.5 mm in diameter. All cores are entirely submitted in one cassette (A1). Representative sections are examined histologically with H&E staining. Additional unstained slides cut and retained for immunohistochemical and molecular studies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Histologic sections from Specimen A reveal cores of pulmonary parenchyma containing an infiltrating epithelial neoplasm. The tumor is composed of atypical columnar to cuboidal cells arranged predominantly in irregular acinar/glandular formations (approximately 60% of tumor area) with a secondary lepidic growth component (approximately 25% of tumor area), characterized by neoplastic cells lining intact alveolar walls without stromal, pleural, or vascular invasion in the lepidic zones. A minor solid component is present (estimated 10-15%). Tumor cells display moderate nuclear pleomorphism with enlarged, irregular nuclei, vesicular chromatin, and prominent, often eccentrically placed nucleoli. The cytoplasm is moderate in amount and pale to finely granular. Mitotic figures are identified (2-3 per 10 high-power fields, with no atypical forms appreciated on limited material). Focal intracytoplasmic mucin is identified (Alcian blue/PAS stain: focally positive). Prominent lymphovascular invasion is identified in one of three cores, with clusters of malignant cells within endothelial-lined channels. No squamous differentiation, no intercellular bridges, no keratin pearl formation. No small cell or neuroendocrine morphology. Background lung parenchyma shows mild anthracotic pigment deposition and mild respiratory bronchiolitis, consistent with smoking history. No evidence of granulomatous inflammation. No organisms identified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
Performed on 4-micron FFPE sections using validated antibody clones and appropriate positive and negative controls. Results are as follows:

  TTF-1 (clone 8G7G3/1):              POSITIVE -- diffuse, strong nuclear staining in >90% of tumor cells
  Napsin-A (polyclonal):               POSITIVE -- diffuse granular cytoplasmic staining in >80% of tumor cells
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):            POSITIVE -- diffuse cytoplasmic staining
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):                 NEGATIVE -- no staining in tumor cells
  p40 (clone BC28):                    NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells
  p63 (clone 4A4):                     NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells
  Synaptophysin (clone MRQ-40):        NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A (clone LK2H10):       NEGATIVE
  CD56 (NCAM1, clone 123C3):          NEGATIVE
  Ki-67 (clone MIB-1):                 Approximately 30% nuclear labeling index

The immunohistochemical profile (TTF-1+, Napsin-A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-, neuroendocrine markers negative) is consistent with pulmonary adenocarcinoma. Squamous cell carcinoma and small cell/large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma are excluded.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES -- MOLECULAR / BIOMARKER TESTING:

1. EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS (cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2, Roche; performed in-house on FFPE material):
   Result: EGFR EXON 21 p.L858R POINT MUTATION DETECTED
   Allele frequency: 38.2%
   EGFR exon 19 deletion: Not detected
   EGFR exon 20 insertion: Not detected
   EGFR T790M (exon 20): Not detected
   KRAS codon 12/13: Not detected (consistent with EGFR-mutant adenocarcinoma)
   Comment: The L858R substitution in exon 21 is a well-characterized activating mutation associated with sensitivity to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Allele frequency of 38.2% is consistent with a clonal alteration in a moderately cellular tumor sample.

2. ALK IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (clone D5F3, Ventana OptiView; FDA-approved companion diagnostic assay):
   Result: NEGATIVE
   Interpretation: No ALK protein overexpression detected. ALK rearrangement is not supported by IHC. Confirmatory FISH not performed per institutional protocol when D5F3 IHC is negative.

3. ROS1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (clone D4D6, Cell Signaling Technology):
   Result: NEGATIVE
   Interpretation: No ROS1 protein overexpression detected. ROS1 rearrangement is not supported. FISH confirmatory testing not indicated.

4. PD-L1 EXPRESSION (Dako PD-L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx; FDA-approved companion diagnostic assay; scoring per tumor proportion score [TPS] methodology):
   Result: TPS 5% (POSITIVE, LOW)
   CPS (Combined Positive Score): 9
   Comment: Tumor proportion score of 5% indicates low PD-L1 expression. This result may be informative in the context of immunotherapy decision-making in combination with other clinical and molecular factors.

5. ADDITIONAL MOLECULAR STUDIES:
   FISH for MET amplification: SENT TO REFERENCE LABORATORY -- RESULTS PENDING (see addendum when available)
   Comprehensive NGS (FoundationOne CDx): SENT TO REFERENCE LABORATORY -- RESULTS PENDING (see addendum when available)
   Comment: Additional molecular characterization is being performed per multidisciplinary oncology team request to provide comprehensive genomic profiling for treatment planning purposes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT UPPER LOBE MASS, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   -- INVASIVE ADENOCARCINOMA, PULMONARY PRIMARY
   -- Mixed acinar (predominant) and lepidic growth pattern, with minor solid component
   -- Moderately differentiated (Grade 2 per IASLC/WHO criteria)
   -- Lymphovascular invasion present
   -- EGFR p.L858R MUTATION DETECTED (exon 21; allele frequency 38.2%; cobas EGFR assay)
   -- ALK IHC (D5F3): NEGATIVE
   -- ROS1 IHC: NEGATIVE
   -- PD-L1 TPS: 5% (22C3 pharmDx)
   -- Mucin production: Focally present (Alcian blue/PAS)
   -- Ki-67 proliferative index: ~30%
   -- MET FISH: PENDING (see addendum)
   -- Comprehensive NGS (FoundationOne CDx): PENDING (see addendum)

COMMENT: The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings confirm the diagnosis of primary pulmonary invasive adenocarcinoma. The acinar/lepidic growth pattern with TTF-1/Napsin-A co-expression is characteristic of lung primary adenocarcinoma. The identification of an EGFR L858R activating mutation in exon 21 at a high allele frequency (38.2%) identifies this tumor as likely highly sensitive to EGFR-targeted tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy. Negative ALK and ROS1 by IHC make these fusions unlikely. Comprehensive NGS results are pending and will be reported in an addendum; these are expected to provide additional co-mutation data and copy number information relevant to treatment selection and prognostication. This case has been reviewed and signed out by the attending pathologist.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Electronic signature verified
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Distribution: Ordering Physician, Thoracic Oncology, Medical Records

*** ADDENDUM: SEE SEPARATE REPORT FOR COMPREHENSIVE NGS (FoundationOne CDx) RESULTS WHEN AVAILABLE ***
*** ADDENDUM: MET FISH RESULT PENDING -- WILL BE ISSUED AS SEPARATE ADDENDUM ***
